entry.title: Re: how to handle too much output template requirement in...
entry.content: Many thanks

entry.title: Re: AIF Interface monitoring in SolMan
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

I have a look at the AIF monitoring in SAP solution manager Interface Monitoring:
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/solution-manager/expert-portal/monitoring-of-integration-scenarios/sap-aif-channel.html

It supports monitor the total number for messages in status Canceled, Error, Warning, In Process, Successful. If this isn't the case, could you please share more detailed information?

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP: Summary of public information available-PART1
entry.content: 99.7% /mon or /yr ?

entry.title: Re: multi-currency pasting -invalid currency
entry.content:
Hi,

The error 'Invalid Currency' appears if:

1. The user enters the value in Debit(FC) and/or Credit(FC) field and do not mention the Currency Code for a Multi Currency G/L Account or BP. The system for a multi currency G/L Account or BP cannot be itself identify which currency user wants to post the entry for.

2. The error can also appear, if the Currency Code taken is not correct. So, for example, if you want to enter USD 100 and instead mention USF 100, the error will appear. The USF is not a currency code defined in the Currencies setup.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: solman_setup - Database hosts when connecting a manag...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

You are correct that in order to view the DB servers in Managed System Configuration, the LMDB must have complete information about the DB, including the "Technical Instances" and "Hosts," unless it is a virtual type of DB. In certain cases, like ORACLE RAC, HANA SR, HANA MDC, etc., there are specific associations that control the behavior, such as SAP_IdenticalDatabaseSystem and SAP_DatabaseReplicationDependency and so on. 

Useful KBA to send DB to SOLMAN: 3232202 - Guided Answer for Troubleshooting for missing or outdated LMDB data suppliers in SAP Solution Manager and SAP Focused Run.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: cds.drafts.deletionTimeout: 5m Is not triggerring draft delete in 5m.
entry.content: I have CAP draft enabled entity and using I have configs "cds.drafts.deletionTimeout: 5m" and "cds.drafts.gc.enabled: false"  I did wait for half an hour and the gc is not called. The garbage collection is called but once in a day and not for the configured time. any idea?

entry.title: Re: Did you say FUE? A definition.
entry.content:
Thank you for this blog.

How about the external auditor? If we give the auditors the access for login S/4HANA with the business scenario "2OO", "External tax audit", the FUE for these auditors should be counted?

entry.title: Re: Synchronous goods receipt and backflush in production order in embedded Extended warehouse manag
entry.content:
Hi,

I have backflushed activated components in Mfg order, need to be posted from Decentralized EWM system with S4H 2023 , If I trigger Mfg order confirmation from S4 using COR6N, how the automated backflush would be posted from Decentralized EWM.

PMRs for backflushed components must have reached already in EWM after release of the Mfg order( Staging triggered).

Need expert opinion.

entry.title: Questions about the Payment Advice
entry.content:
I have two questions about the payment notice.
1.How do I download payment advice from multiple suppliers at once?
2.How do I download the payment notice in CSV.

entry.title: How to add reviewer in Manage Workflows for Purchase Order?
entry.content:
Hello,

In S/4HANA Public cloud, We need to add reviewer in Manage workflows for Purchase Order, but do not have this option.

But this can be done in Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions.

Best Regards

Sridhar

entry.title: Questions about bank charge contribution codes
entry.content: The Supplier Invoice Registration App is missing the Bank Charge Contribution Codes 1, 2 and 3. What should I do to display it? If it cannot be displayed, where can I enter it?

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Major shifts in SAP Fiori implementation
entry.content:
Upgrading your SAP S/4HANA is often a rare opportunity to shift your user experience fundamentals.  Knowing which shifts to make with your technical upgrade or post-upgrade simplifies planning effort and resources. 

Image of a forklift moving a container - Image source: SAP 

In this post are some practical tips on timing when to shift assuming:

  • your source system is a SAP S/4HANA 2022 or lower, and
  • your target SAP S/4HANA system is SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher

These are the major shifts in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA user experience as at SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher:

  • Move from standalone (hub) FES to embedded FES
  • Move custom technical catalogs from Launchpad Designer to Launchpad App Manager
  • Move launchpad layouts from groups to spaces and pages
  • Move to SAP Build Work Zone, including the optional move to SAP Mobile Start.

Hint: This blog post is relevant for customers running SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.  For example: customers running RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition tailored option. This blog post is also relevant for customers running the SAP S/4HANA product on other infrastructure such as on-premise server or hyperscaler.

 

Moving from standalone (hub) FES to embedded (FES)

Important: You only need to do this move if you are NOT already using embedded FES. Most RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition will already be using embedded FES. If you are not sure, check with your technical team.

A very simplified diagram below shows the difference. Essentially:

  • With standalone FES your SAP Fiori front-end server is a separate server to your SAP S/4HANA server
  • With embedded FES your SAP Fiori front-end server is a software component of your SAP S/4HANA server

 

RECOMMENDATION: Do WITH your technical upgrade

 

Why move to embedded FES as part of technical upgrade 

While the benefits are considerable, this is still a disruptive architectural shift. You will need to:

  • Add new components to your SAP S/4HANA server
  • Adjust sizing on your SAP S/4HANA server for the additional usage
  • Adjust configuration on your SAP S/4HANA server and Web Dispatcher
  • You may need to adjust some integrations, for example move standalone apps to SAP Build Work Zone
  • Re-deploy any custom-built apps to your SAP S/4HANA server or to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • Rework business-as-usual support procedures for security authorizations transports, maintenance, and troubleshooting
  • Retire your existing SAP Fiori front-end server

Embedded FES is planned to be the only option for SAP Fiori front-end server deployment for SAP S/4HANA from SAP S/4HANA 2025.

Refer to:

  • Blog - SAP Fiori deployment options and SAP Fiori front-end server strategy - UPDATE 2023
  • SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations <Fiori landscape and deployment)

Embedded FES reduces system landscape complexity:

  • By installing the SAP Fiori front-end server (FES) directly on the (backend) SAP S/4HANA server
  • By applying strict release relationships between FES and backend server

Embedded FES typically reduces architecture costs:

  • Due to a reduced number of servers in the landscape
  • You also reduce associated operational costs, such as patching, managing transports, and managing security authorizations.
  • You should note that the backend SAP S/4HANA server may need to be resized to cover the additional frontend usage. Refer to SAP Quick Sizer tool and guidelines.

Refer to:

  • SAP Sizing – Quick Sizer guide
  • SAP Help - Quick Sizer Tool
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori Overview – Plan your SAP Fiori implementation project
  • SAP Note 2467172 - How to size Fiori applications based on number of users

Embedded FES provides agility-at-scale by providing rapid activation task lists and other improved operational tooling. that reduce effort and speed up managing your SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA content. For example,

  • Task lists for setting up the Fiori launchpad, activating/updating SAP and custom content, updating security authorizations for custom business roles
  • Tools for managing content at scale, including launchpad app manager, launchpad content manager, manage launchpad spaces and manage launchpad pages
  • Simplified configuration, for launchpad features, for the user help provided by SAP Companion (and optionally SAP Enable Now)
  • Improved diagnostics and performance monitoring, via tools such as App Support, and STATS_FE

Customer experiences suggest that this shift is easiest to manage as part of a technical upgrade. Expect:

  • major impacts on your support team, particularly basis administrators and security authorization administrators
  • minor to moderate impacts on your business users, such as the loss of some personalization

 

How to move to embedded FES 

Option 1: Do a formal transition from standalone (hub) FES to embedded FES.

Refer to:

  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori Overview – Update, Upgrade, and Migrate
  • Blog - SAP Fiori transition from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP S/4HANA
  • Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Option 2: Consider setting up embedded FES from scratch.

This can be a good option that saves time, especially if you currently use less than 100 SAP Fiori apps, including custom-built apps based on SAP Fiori UX, such as SAP Fiori design, SAP Fiori elements, and/or SAPUI5 development.

You have the advantage of using new rapid activation tools immediately to set up your embedded FES to current best practices.

To migrate your current content, consider that:

  • Custom-built apps can be exported/imported to change where they are stored (i.e. to move from one SAPUI5 ABAP Repository to another, or using SAP Fiori Tools to re-deploy the app to SAP BTP)
  • Fiori launchpad personalization can be exported/imported.
  • There are some restrictions on moving app views (aka app variants) – read the Additional considerations at the end of this section.

Refer to:

  • SAPUI5 SDK - Using the SAPUI5 ABAP Repository Upload and Download Reports to Synchronize
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori Tools – Deploying an application
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad administration guide – section Migrate Personalization Data
  • SAP Note 2789848 - Migration report for homepage and application personalization

Option 3: You can use a Hybrid approach that includes a move to SAP Build Work Zone alongside option 1 or 2.

This can be useful if you have a large number of custom-built apps, 3rd party apps, standalone (non-S/4HANA) apps), or SAP Cloud solutions that you want to manage side-by-side on SAP Build Work Zone.

Important: SAP Build Work Zone is NOT a replacement of embedded FES for SAP S/4HANA. Your SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA content must still be deployed on your SAP S/4HANA server and federated to SAP Build Work Zone via content exposure from SAP S/4HANA.

  • Refer to: Blog - SAP Fiori deployment options and SAP Fiori front-end server strategy - UPDATE 2023
  • SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations <Fiori landscape and deployment)
  • SAP Help – SAP Build Work Zone - Federation of Remote Content Providers
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad administration guide – Exposing launchpad content to SAP BTP

 

Additional considerations for moving to embedded FES

Considerations for your support team

Make sure your team is skilled up in the latest recommended tools and processes. In most cases, moving to the embedded server provides many opportunities to simplify and speed up business-as-usual support. For example, by using rapid content activation for SAP Business Roles and custom business roles.

Considerations for your business users

Set an expectation that some user personalization is likely to be lost.  There is some migration tooling for personalization however customer experiences so far suggest that almost always you lose some personalization. The degree of loss depends on the number of apps used, personalization features used, and the difference between your source and target SAP S/4HANA releases. 

Be aware that there some restrictions on moving app views (aka app variants), partly due to lifecycle shifts in how public views are managed. You may want to take the opportunity to downgrade public views to private views where needed. You can explicitly recreate new public views using Adapt UI. Refer to:

  • SAP Note 3019518 - Report to convert customer variants to private variants
  • Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Yes Key Users can create default app settings for filters, tables, and charts

How to evaluate your current app usage

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition and on-premise customers can find some statistics on your current app usage through SAP Early Watch. Refer to:

  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Dashboard - New statistics about "Used SAPUI5 Applications"

 

Technical Catalog Migration – moving technical catalogs from Launchpad Designer to Launchpad App Manager

Important: You only need to do this move if you have created custom technical catalogs using the Launchpad Designer on SAP S/4HANA 2022 or lower releases and are now upgrading to SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher.

Customers starting on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher are recommended to use Launchpad app manager only.  You should avoid using Launchpad Designer.

 

RECOMMENDATION: Do WITH your technical upgrade

 

Why migrate technical catalogs with your technical upgrade

This is a minor technical shift which is quick to complete. Typical expected effort is a few hours to a few days, depending on the number of custom catalogs created.

Moving to the launchpad app manager makes business-as-usual support and maintenance of launchpad content easier. That is, the launchpad app manager provides features that support agility at scale, such as:

  • a strict alignment of tiles and target mappings
  • adaptation features to maximize reuse of SAP technical catalogs

There is typically no impact on business users, so it is difficult to justify the activity as a separate functional project.

 

How to migrate technical catalogs

Run the Technical Catalog Migration tool across all of your custom technical catalogs.

Refer to:

  • SAP Note 3444719 - Documentation Correction: Missing Topics in SAP Fiori Launchpad Guide (SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01)
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – Why and Getting Ready for Migration
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – How the migration process works

 

Additional considerations for technical catalog migration

There can be extra effort involved if you do not have a clear separation between your cross-client custom technical catalogs and your client-specific custom business catalogs. For example, if you have custom technical catalogs assigned directly to business roles, or if you have separated tiles and target mappings into different catalogs.

The migration tool will help identify catalogs that need to be adjusted prior to migration.

 

Interim measures on your source SAP S/4HANA release to minimize migration effort on upgrade

  • If you have a single-level catalog design, start to split out into technical catalogs (cross-client) and business catalogs (client-specific)
  • Only create new custom technical catalogs in Launchpad App Manager
  • Avoid rework by locking as many people as possible out of Launchpad Designer

 

Moving from Groups to Spaces and Pages

Important: You only need to do this move if you are currently using Groups layout mode for your launchpad and are now upgrading to SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher. 

Customers starting on SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher are recommended to use spaces and pages only.

Be aware that functionality and features will depend on your SAP S/4HANA release. For example, new My Home page is available from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

 

RECOMMENDATION: Do Post-Upgrade

 

Image: Summary of Groups versus Spaces and pages with My Home - key differences and benefits as at SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. Source: SAP.

 

Why move to spaces and pages post-upgrade

There are significant, mostly positive, impacts on business users:

  • Provide lean, consistent, hierarchical pages that reflect your work responsibilities
  • More tile size options to reduce whitespace
  • Improved personalization options, such as space personalization to hide/show and sort space tabs
  • User personalizations automatically merged with administrator changes, supporting process changes over time
  • Option for merged spaces or pages for complex business roles
  • Improved performance, as only the current page is loaded at runtime

Image: Key benefits for spaces and pages for the business user.  Image source: SAP.

You have better process control by using spaces and pages to reflect desire work practices:

  • Reduced training for new recruits, because spaces and pages reflect job responsibilities
  • Reduced re-training for existing staff after process changes, because administrator changes to spaces and pages reflect process changes over item
  • For example, some customers use spaces, pages, and tiles to reinforce their BPMN process models. When processes change, layouts can be adjusted to reinforce the process change. This simplifies change management activities.

Hint: From SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, spaces and pages include the very attractive new Home page. In other words, it is an obvious disadvantage to be using Groups layout from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

Image: Spaces and pages in a nutshell - key features and behaviours. Image source: SAP.

 

There are significant, mostly positive, operational impacts on administering security authorizations:

  • Spaces (and their assigned pages) can be reused across multiple roles, including composite roles
  • WYSIWYG editors and role-context preview checks to support role alignment
  • Administrator changes automatically merged with user personalizations, supporting process changes over time
  • Read-only report Overview of Roles, Spaces, and Pages that can be shared with business stakeholders and auditors
  • Where-used support in maintenance tools such as launchpad content manager

Image: Key benefits of spaces and pages for administrators. Image source: SAP.

Step-wise user-by-user or role-by-role move is supported. This is definitely worth discussing with your business sponsors and stakeholders:

  • Stages are controlled by launchpad configuration parameters
  • You can use the “Enable Spaces” setting option to permit users to toggle between groups and spaces for piloting purposes
  • To speed up your move, you can use SAP delivered spaces and pages as a starting point; create your own custom spaces and pages; or convert current groups to spaces and pages via the migration tool.

 

How to move to spaces and pages post-upgrade

You should consider how you will govern your spaces and pages design. This maximizes business effectiveness, minimizes rework; minimizes maintenance effort, and reduces training for business users. 

For example,

  • Consider who should be responsible/accountable/consulted/informed for administrator-controlled space and page content. For example, you may want your business process experts to take ownership of the page design.
  • Consider how spaces and pages will be updated as process changes are introduced
  • Consider how spaces and pages will be updated when you upgrade to future releases

You should discuss with your current business stakeholders:

  • Whether current groups are meeting business needs, i.e. are they worth the migration effort
  • Whether SAP delivered spaces and pages for equivalent business roles are an effective starting point for business needs
  • Whether a fully custom design is needed, and how to minimize the effort of this approach
  • Discuss whether the business should take ownership of page layouts. You have the option to grow this ownership over time. For example:
    • Initial review and sign-off
    • Proposing changes to layouts and testing them
    • Making changes to layouts as a key user (technical team would still have responsibility for transports)

For any custom-built spaces and pages, you should use a templated approach to the id, title, and content of spaces and pages.

For example:

  • Consider using space and page templates, where available for your release
  • Consider naming conventions for space/page ids for the administrator
  • Consider naming conventions for space/page titles for users
  • SAP delivered spaces typically include an Overview page for daily tasks
  • SAP delivered spaces typically include one or more pages named for regular business processes, e.g. Purchase Requisition Processing
  • SAP delivered spaces may also include pages named for seasonal or exception processes, e.g. Tax Declaration

 

Additional considerations for moving to spaces and pages

You should consider moving users step-wise to spread the migration effort over time.  You can use the launchpad configuration parameters to control default features. For example:

  • Leave your current groups as-is. There is no need to delete them. 
  • Technically activate the spaces features with your technical upgrade or immediately after upgrade, but leave “groups” as the default for all users
  • Move first users who will be assigned urgent new innovations.
    • For example, you can Enable Spaces settings option to enable users to toggle between groups and spaces while they get used to the new layouts
  • Defer moving users whose current roles have no or minimal changes
    • Consider a simple templated approach for these users
  • Once all users are moved make spaces the default.

Refer to:

  • Blog - Getting Started with Spaces and Pages
  • Blog - Merging Spaces and Pages
  • Blog - Info Package: Making the Move from Launchpad Groups to Spaces (In SAP S/4HANA and in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition)

Interim measures on your source SAP S/4HANA release to minimize effort on upgrade

You can start moving role-by-role earlier, if you are on SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher.

You can technically activate spaces and pages in your current release, but leave groups as the default. This enables you to start to exploring the main features and review differences in your target SAP S/4HANA release.

Hint: From SAP S/4HANA 2020 it is technically possible to use spaces and pages.  However for most customers is likely to be too early as there are no SAP-delivered spaces and pages for business roles as a starting point, and only basic features available.

 

Moving to SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start

 

RECOMMENDATION: This move is optional and can be done any time.  You may want to defer this shift to post-upgrade.

 

Image: Example screenshots of the entry page to SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start. Image source: SAP.

 

Why move to SAP Build Work Zone post-upgrade

SAP Build Work Zone provides an additional layer to:

  • Provide internet access to selected roles
  • Provide mobile access to selected roles
  • Provide external partner access to selected roles
  • Provide access across multiple cloud and on-premise solutions

Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

You may need to consider commercial aspects, such as the use of your RISE BTP credits.

You will need to consider architecture and cyber security aspects.

You will need to consider current restrictions, such as which content can be exposed, and which content can be federated from SAP S/4HANA. Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone - Restrictions

  • Be aware that these restrictions change frequently so review the roadmap when making plans.
  • You need to review content exposure options and implications. For example, using Spaces and Pages versus Spaces and Pages – New Experience

Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Exposing Launchpad Content to SAP BTP

 

How to move to SAP Build Work Zone

You will need to configure the SAP Build Work Zone site to support your use cases.

You will need to federate the content from SAP S/4HANA using the content exposure. Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone - Integrating Business Content and SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Exposing Launchpad Content to SAP BTP

You will need to test your configuration from multiple entry points including:

  • Different web browsers
  • Different device types
  • Different user types – e.g. internal versus external

 

Additional considerations for moving to SAP Mobile Start

SAP Mobile Start is an optional component to provide a native launchpad for mobile devices on Apple (iOS) or Android. Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Mobile Start

You will need to consider mobile device types supported and any current restrictions.
You should update your mobile device strategy to cover any impacts to your operations, such as procedures for deploying SAP Mobile Start.

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: Changes in Workflow is not reflecting in quality system
entry.content: Hi Priyanka,This is expected behavior. Workflow steps will be executed based on the version they were started with. The reason it worked in Development but not in Quality is that in Development, you might have made the latest changes to your current version. A new version might have been generated upon activation and moved to the Quality system... Typically the transport will carry only the latest changes

entry.title: Re: calculate GL cumulative balance in GR55 report
entry.content: Solved

entry.title: Re: error w while trying to create a blog
entry.content:
Replying here to see if this might help unlock the ability to ask questions.
NOTE FOR OTHERS: There's a wait time before you can create Q&A or Posts.

Gaurav

entry.title: Re: HXEDownloadManager can't run on windows 10
entry.content:
Hi Dan_vL，

Thanks a lot for your reply.

My Windows PC have already install JRE environment as below shows:

>java -version
openjdk version "21.0.2" 2024-01-16 LTS
OpenJDK Runtime Environment Microsoft-8905927 (build 21.0.2+13-LTS)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Microsoft-8905927 (build 21.0.2+13-LTS, mixed mode, sharing)

But when run HXE download manager, it always pops up below error. And also I tested to run other jar files, which works fine on my windows PC.

> java -jar HXEDownloadManager.jar
Cannot open log file C:\Users\[Username]\AppData\Local\Temp\\hxedm240904.log.

One suspicious point is the [username] part is not complete but with character ~, wonder how to fix this issue.

thanks again.

entry.title: Re: object page as first page in fiori elements
entry.content:
Hi @GowthamRaja 
I have a same requirement to navigate to OP first. I've tried a lot using inbound navigation but no luck.
Could you please help with how you've changed routes accordingly to navigate to object page of a specific record first bypassing list report?

Thank you in advance.!!

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:

Here the completion picture of the learning journey:

I hope that you will consider my request. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: GST Margin scheme on second hand material purchases
entry.content: Thank you Lakshmipathi, could you please suggest is this condition type for margin scheme to be added in PO pricing schema or it has to be added in TAXINN pricing procedure. Regards Prakash

entry.title: Re: Possibilities of Cost Center
entry.content: Thanks, got the solution with your help

entry.title: Re: Rejected RFQ
entry.content:
The Hierarchy of Release Strategy has rejected the RFQ. The status of RFQ in the system is Rejected. The above settings will block the user from creating a PO of RFQ having rejected status.

 

Ali Zaidi 
SD / MM

entry.title: Re: setting data with SetDataSource in c#
entry.content: In designer it works, the report is generated, the subreport query is called for each record from the master. However, I want to do the same thing from the c# code using SetDataSource. I want to execute the SQL query myself, build the DataTable, DataSet and set it in the report using SetDataSource. Unfortunately, I don't know how to do it? It looks like I have to call SetDataSource with a new row for each generated page (How can I catch a page change in c# without CrystalReportViewer?). I also don't want to open every report I have with Designer and change anything.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
hi David.

Myself Usman Interested in learn and get certification 

working as a end user in in pakistan want to upskill myself 

regards,

M Usman

entry.title: Difference between custom report and fbl3n data results
entry.content:
left (ALV custom report result) right (fbl3n tcode result)

when matched there are hkont (GL/Account) & belnr (DcoumentNo) fields that do not appear in the ALV custom report, even though the query has pulled the others, what do you think is the reason? here is my code :

FORM fetch_data.

  " show data based on PERIOD or POSTING DATE
  IF p_period = 'X'. "Jika p_period is true

    SELECT bukrs belnr gjahr budat monat waers hwaer blart bldat
     INTO TABLE i_bkpf
     FROM bkpf AS a
     WHERE a~gjahr = s_fiscyr " fiscal year
         AND bukrs = '1000' " company code
         AND monat IN s_perio. " period

  ELSE. " Posting Date

    SELECT bukrs belnr gjahr budat monat waers hwaer blart bldat
      INTO TABLE i_bkpf
      FROM bkpf AS a
      WHERE a~gjahr = s_fiscyr " fiscal year
          AND bukrs = '1000' " company code
          AND budat BETWEEN s_date-low AND s_date-high. " Posting date
  ENDIF.

  IF i_bkpf IS NOT INITIAL.

          " GET DATA FROM BSEG TABLE
      SELECT hkont bukrs belnr gjahr bschl lifnr ebeln wrbtr prctr aufnr
      INTO TABLE i_bseg
      FROM bseg
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN i_bkpf
        WHERE gjahr = i_bkpf-gjahr
          AND bukrs = i_bkpf-bukrs
          AND belnr = i_bkpf-belnr " Document Number
          AND hkont IN s_hkont.

    " GET VENDOR DATA
    IF i_bseg IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT lifnr name1 stras ort01 pstlz
      INTO TABLE i_lfnr
      FROM lfa1
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN i_bseg
        WHERE lifnr = i_bseg-lifnr.
    ENDIF.

    IF i_bseg IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT e~ebeln e~bedat e~lifnr f~matnr f~menge f~netpr
        INTO TABLE i_ekkp
         FROM ekko AS e
         LEFT JOIN ekpo AS f
         ON e~ebeln = f~ebeln " Purchasing Document
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN i_bseg
        WHERE lifnr = i_bseg-lifnr
        AND e~ebeln = i_bseg-ebeln.

    ENDIF.

    IF i_bseg IS NOT INITIAL.

      LOOP AT i_bkpf INTO ls_bkpf.
        LOOP AT i_bseg INTO ls_bseg WHERE bukrs = ls_bkpf-bukrs AND gjahr = ls_bkpf-gjahr AND belnr = ls_bkpf-belnr.
          CLEAR ls_result.

          IF ls_bseg-wrbtr IS NOT INITIAL.

            ls_bseg-wrbtr = ls_bseg-wrbtr * 100.

          ENDIF.

          IF ls_bseg-lifnr IS NOT INITIAL.
            READ TABLE i_lfnr INTO ls_lfnr WITH KEY lifnr = ls_bseg-lifnr BINARY SEARCH.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_lfnr TO ls_result.
          ENDIF.

          IF ls_bseg-ebeln IS NOT INITIAL.
            READ TABLE i_ekkp INTO ls_ekkp WITH KEY ebeln = ls_bseg-ebeln BINARY SEARCH.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_ekkp TO ls_result.
          ENDIF.

          IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_bkpf TO ls_result.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_bseg TO ls_result.
            APPEND ls_result TO lt_result.
          ENDIF.

        ENDLOOP.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

    IF p_sum_gl = 'X'.
      PERFORM display_sum_gl.
    ENDIF.

    IF p_sum_vn = 'X'.
      PERFORM display_sum_vn.
    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.                    "fetch_data

entry.title: Re: Working with RF Transactions in the Warehouse
entry.content:
Hi Gheorghe,

I am working with a situation similar to your example 1 however my warehouse order is the withdrawal step of 2-step picking and all tasks have the same destination storage bin. I have configured auto creation of the allocation step WTs. Before I have picked all of the HUs in the withdrawal step I have another resource that needs to start moving the HUs from the destination bin (begin the allocation step WTs).

How can I confirm the destination bin for the first WT before picking the second WT?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Rework Processing - Stock-Manufactured Material (BJN) & Serial Numbers
entry.content:
When I use the scope item BDD - Customer Returns as input for the scope item BJN, I already have a serial number from  the returned good for FG126. I use this material as a BoM item in my rework order and also as the header material.

In the standard process, a new serial number for FG126 is created, which doesn’t seem right to me because I would like to use the input serial number at the header level for my goods receipt of the reworked product.

Could you please give me some ideas on how to handle such a use case? Are there any possibilities in customizing the serial number profile based on the production order type?

entry.title: How to create and authorize users in HDI Containers ?
entry.content:
Hi,

I use a HDI container on HANA Cloud from a Cloud Foundry app in python to perform machine learning operations. The results are stored in the corresponding HDI container schema.

I want to access the schema from a SAP Analytics Cloud via live model access.

This works fine for a connection to HANA using the users from the service keys from the HDI container.

Now the requirement is to assign SAC users certain rights depending on the dimensions. Since this seems to be impossible on the SAC, we would need to create users with these rights in the HANA HDI container. How can I create and maintain such users from the CF environment ? 

Any help is highly appreciated.

Regards

Marcus

entry.title: Re: Extract attributes from XML
entry.content:
Hi, try:

/SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/ERPNumber
/SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/ProcurementUnit/UniqueName
/SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/UniqueName

entry.title: Re: How to view and change the settlement profile for a p...
entry.content:
Hi @alexisme ,

Settlement Profile can be viewed in the following customization path, Accounting > Overhead Costs > Overhead Cost Processes > Period End closing > Create Settlement Profile

 

 

Settlement Rule assigned to a production order can be viewed from fiori app 'Display Production Order (CO03)' using the following path Menu > Header > Settlement Rule. Once you click on Settlement rule, it directs you to the new tab for Order-Event based where it displays the posting rule details. 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Prabha A

entry.content:

This is to inform that the feature package 2408 has been released on 24/08/2024 and includes the fix of the scenario described in SAP Note 3446134 

Could you please check issue in 2408.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One support

entry.title: Re: Planned Order Process-( PP -EWM Integration)

entry.title: HANA DR setup with cost efficient setup (w/o HSR)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to build a DR for our RH1 system (SAP NETWEAVER/HANA 2.00.055). DR in different zone/region
As there are cost constraints, we can't build a HANA DB with same or partial memory with preload off.

Can you please suggest a way if we can proceed with using any scenarios using below methods other than HSR:
1) Full Backup or Differential backup, copied in routine with its transaction logs at DR site on storage.
OR
2) Copying the HANA DATA/LOG Volume to a storage in DR, and then to use it during DR call.
OR
Any other cost-efficient method where storage at DR can be used for copying file in any of the above-mentioned methods.
But Physical memory of HANA is not involved.

Warm regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HANA DR setup with cost efficient setup (w/o HSR)
entry.content:
Dear customer,

I understand you concern is to copy the tenant database to another system.
If it's correct, you can copy the tenant database in various ways including backup and recovery below into another system.
Could you please first refer to 'Copying and Moving Tenant Databases' using system replication. 
Please see the copy mechanism of this method from the admin guide above, along with the other related information.

And then, you can complete this task by using a backup and recovery operation, and please refer to the following admin guides.
Points to Note: Copying a Database Using Backup and Recovery 
Copying a Database Using Backup and Recovery 
Copying a Database Using Backup and Recovery(HANA Cockpit)

In addition, please take a look at SAP Note 2039883 - FAQ: SAP HANA database and data snapshots (storage snapshots) for the detail information about storage snapshot.  

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Connect SAP on premise system with Google Drive so that we can call API's using ABAP
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have some files stored in a google drive and I want to access these files from SAP using ABAP. I have created the client id and secret and also tested the authentication token with postman and it seems to be working fine in Postman. Now my question is that how to call the google drive API like "https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files" from SAP using ABAP. Also how to get the auth token.

I have found one resource which talks about the same but I am not able to achieve it as SAP blocks HTTPS communication with google without SSL and we don't have the signed SSL certificate for it. Also do I need ABAP SDK for google cloud for achieving this?

Resource -> https://cloud.google.com/solutions/sap/docs/abap-sdk/on-premises-or-any-cloud/latest/authentication-oauth-client-credentials

Is there any way to communicate without SSL or by using HTTP rather than HTTPS?

Kindly help with this.

So i cant not convert the Planned order into Production Order. But in the links you shared i found the process begins with Production order. Following is the Blog i found to execute the Repetitive manufacturing with Planned order. But as i mentioned above While Executing MF60 when i click on STAGE the status of material is not changed to green and the save option is not available. Do you have any other thoughts or information to execute this process, please do share. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Need to add custom fields in APP - Repost Line Items...
entry.content: Reposting line items in cost accounting is crucial for financial accuracy. Start by identifying any inaccuracies in cost entries. Then, adjust and repost these entries with the correct values. It's important to document the reasons for these adjustments and obtain the necessary approvals. Afterward, reconcile the accounts to ensure that financial statements reflect true costs. Maintaining an audit trail by recording both original and adjusted entries is essential for transparency. This process ensures compliance with accounting standards, enhances accuracy, and supports informed decision-making. For more financial insights, visit rosemarywater.site.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
Hi All,

1. If we deactivates the 08 inspection type from that material, inspection lot will not be created but it will not work as client performs plant to plant stock transfer process too.

2. Even if we de-activate the quality inspection at 453 & 311 movement types, still its generating the inspection lot as it is S/4 to EWM stock transfer process at the time of Warehouse task generation.

3. However  if we de-activate the quality inspection at 453 & 311 movement types, the Inspection lot will not be generated in S/4 Hana system.

4. Only Solution i can see is the Enhancement, i.e. whenever the inbound delivery is created and once the warehouse task generates, system will check the movement types (453/311) in Table, if those movement types available there in that table, system will not generate the Inspection lots.

Thanks You

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 20 - Quality Management with SAP EWM
entry.content: Great explanation!

entry.title: Re: Extract attributes from XML
entry.content:
Hi Rakesh, 

There are multiple ways to achieve your requirement to extract the values as requested. 

The simplest among them would be to use a Content Modifier and extract the attribute values to be stored in properties. This can be achieved using creating properties with relevant XPath expressions. We can create three properties by configuring XPaths as below for the Content Modifier

  • For ERPNumber:

    • Name: ERPNumber
    • Type: XPath
    • Value: Use the XPath expression /SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/ERPNumber

  • For ProcurementUnit_UniqueName:

    • Name: ProcurementUnit_UniqueName
    • Type: XPath
    • Value: Use the XPath expression /SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/ProcurementUnit/UniqueName

  • For UniqueName (directly under item):

    • Name: UniqueName
    • Type: XPath
    • Value: Use the XPath expression /SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/UniqueName

 

 

Alternatively, you can use groovy script or XSLT transformation if you have some other extra steps where you want to use the extracted values instead of storing in properties for further usage.

Srini

Srinivas Bhumireddy

entry.title: Re: Remittance advice gone customer clerk internet addres...
entry.content:
Hi Tazammul,

Thank you for your question. Based on your description, it seems the issue may arise if the customer information in the payment data is not initialized.

Currently, the default logic for determining the email address for payment advice depends on the role settings defined in the business rules within Output Parameter Determination. If the supplier information in the payment details is not initial (i.e., not empty), the receiver's role is designated as H (supplier), and the system fetches the email address from the supplier master data. Similarly, if the customer information is not initial, the system retrieves the email address from the customer master data. 

I hope this clarifies the issue for you. Additionally, you can use the "Addition of Email Recipients" Business Add-In (BAdI) to implement custom logic, which enables the payment advice output to be sent to multiple email addresses, including 'TO' and 'CC' recipients. For more detailed information, please refer to the documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

Kind Regards,

Sharon

entry.title: Re: Registrations Now Open for SAP Inside Track Gurugram 2024 - Business AI Edition
entry.content:

Saurabh Gupta

entry.title: Re: Technical details - HANA licensing
entry.content:
Hi Srikanth,

as for your export of users: This is indirect usage. Whether you need an additional license, depends on how you export the users. The way you describe the use case you are using standard S/4 functionality, which is good. If you schedule the export to run automatically and follow some guidelines, you should be able to export them without the need of a license. 

I guess having a look at the "Indirect Static Read" section of this document will help you: Indirect_Access_Guide_for_SAP_Installed_Base.pdf

If you create users by reading in a csv file via SAP standard functionality, you should be fine and not require an additional license for that (apart from the Named User License of whoever does it).

Best regards,

Torben

entry.title: Re: Mandatory cds filters missing in Fiori Overview Page...
entry.content:
Hi Harish,

Did you find the solution for OVP ? we have similar requirement bug we are getting error. Kinldy share your inputs

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
With this remark from you I think it's related to another enhancement that is triggering the lot: "2. Even if we de-activate the quality inspection at 453 & 311 movement types, still its generating the inspection lot as it is S/4 to EWM stock transfer process at the time of Warehouse task generation." Maybe investigate that first before making an enhancement on the QM side.

Also still think the 453 movement should not be the movement that triggers the stock transfer/inbound delivery creation to EWM. This should be the initial receipt of the customer return with a 651 movement. If that movement is received in EWM with an inbound delivery there should be no inbound delivery creation when the 453 movement is done, unless you are moving this between different plants when the 453 is posted.

entry.title: how to call HANA SP from Node with input and output parameters
entry.content:
I’m working on a Node.js application where I need to call an SAP HANA stored procedure that has both input and output parameters. The stored procedure performs various operations based on the input parameters and returns output parameters that I need to capture in my Node.js code. While I’ve managed to set up the basic connection to the HANA database using the @Sap/hana-client library, I’m encountering issues when trying to pass input parameters and retrieve the output parameters correctly. Specifically, I’m seeing errors like "Too many parameters for the SQL statement" or difficulties in accessing the output values after the procedure is executed. Could anyone provide guidance or examples on how to correctly call a HANA stored procedure from Node.js, handle both input and output parameters, and retrieve the output values efficiently?

CREATE PROCEDURE schema.GETNORANGE(

IN i_NORANGECD VARCHAR(10),

IN i_USERID VARCHAR(12),

OUT p_RETURN_NO VARCHAR(50),

OUT p_RETTYPE VARCHAR(1),

OUT p_RETMSG VARCHAR(500)

)

Hello Team, 

We are implementing FI, SD , MM  modules by using Grow with SAP S4 HANA (Public cloud)  for one of our clients. This is migration project where we are migrating from ECC system to S4 HANA.

Now our client wants to implement HR modules which will be suitable with Grow with SAP S4 HANA (Public cloud) therefore need your assistance.

  1. Can we add HR module with Grow with SAP S4 HANA (Public cloud)?
  2. Do we need to pay separate license cost ?
  3. What is the different between Grow with SAP S4 HANA (Public cloud) and successfcators?

Thank You, 

Rini Mukherjee 

+91-9038870937

entry.title: Re: SAP Application Migration Assistant
entry.content:
I have been using the migration assistant and it is very hit and miss. It (sometimes) works for small simple packages / delivery units but it struggles with real life example (Gets stuck on XS Migration: Starting Processing).

And has only read the objects in and seems to time out:

Is there a VS Code add-on so i can run it locally?

entry.title: Re: Facing 401 unauthorized error while trying post data...
entry.content: You configuration is not correct. The response should be 200; Message OK. Use the hostname and credentials from the key for plan api from service SAP Process Integration Runtime. This provider is for discovery. When you configure the iflow later, you need to provide the credentials from the plan integration-flow. Warning: The iflow has to be of type API (REST or oData) for discovery to list these.

entry.title: call a SAP HANA stored procedure that returns multiple result sets (i.e., multiple tables)
entry.content: I'm working on a Node.js application where I need to call a SAP HANA stored procedure that returns multiple result sets (i.e., multiple tables). The stored procedure is designed to return different sets of data based on various queries, and I need to capture all these result sets in my Node.js code. I’m using the @Sap/hana-client library to connect to the HANA database. However, I'm facing challenges in retrieving and handling these multiple result sets after the stored procedure execution. Could anyone provide guidance or examples on how to properly call an SAP HANA stored procedure from Node.js and process multiple result sets returned by the procedure?

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management : Provisi...
entry.content:
Hi Chong,

In S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. the planning of project costs is majorly done via the excel upload. the planning of costs is done at WBS element level period wise. As per the below template available you can plan the costs for whichever period you wanted by re uploading the excel with the additional amounts.

Regards,

Sai

entry.title: Re: Regarding HR module
entry.content:
Hi Rini,

if you want to use HR with GROW, you would license the following product: "GROW with SAP, core HR add-on"

The supplemental terms and conditions can be found here: https://www.sap.com/docs/download/agreements/product-use-and-support-terms/cls/en/grow-with-sap-core-hr-add-on-supplement-english-v4-2024.pdf

This product comes with a price tag, so there will be additional costs.

Technically this is nothing but integrated SuccessFactors. SAP created this bundle to commercially make it more attractive to GROW customers.

Best regards,

Torben

entry.title: File Transfer from SAP ECC to S3 bucket using AWS SDK
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to establish file transfer from SAP ECC to S3 bucket using AWS SDK.
Kindly help to suggest the prerequisites and setup procedure for the same.

Regards,



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: File Transfer from SAP ECC to S3 bucket using AWS SDK
entry.content:
To establish a file transfer from SAP ECC to an Amazon S3 bucket using the AWS SDK, you'll need to follow several steps to set up the necessary components and permissions. Below is a high-level overview of the prerequisites and setup procedure:

Prerequisites:

  1. AWS Account: You need an active AWS account with access to S3 services.

  2. S3 Bucket: Create an S3 bucket in your AWS account where the files will be uploaded.

  3. IAM User: Create an IAM user with programmatic access and attach a policy that grants the necessary permissions to upload files to the S3 bucket.

  4. AWS SDK: Install the AWS SDK on the SAP ECC server. For Java-based applications, this would be the AWS SDK for Java. For ABAP, you might need to use the AWS SDK for JavaScript or Python and call it from SAP ECC.

  5. SAP ECC Configuration: Ensure that your SAP ECC system can make outbound HTTP/HTTPS calls if you're using a RESTful approach or that it can execute OS-level commands if you're using the SDK through a command-line interface.

  6. Network Configuration: Configure your network to allow connections from the SAP ECC server to AWS S3 endpoints.

Setup Procedure:

  1. IAM Policy and User:

    • Create an IAM policy that grants access to the S3 bucket for operations like s3:PutObject.
    • Create an IAM user and attach the policy to this user.
    • Note down the Access Key ID and Secret Access Key for the IAM user.

  2. Install AWS SDK:

    • Depending on your SAP ECC environment, install the appropriate AWS SDK.
    • For Java, you can include the SDK as a library in your Java-based applications.
    • For ABAP, you might need to use external commands or RFC to interact with the SDK.

  3. Configure AWS SDK:

    • Set up the AWS SDK with the credentials (Access Key ID and Secret Access Key) of the IAM user.
    • Configure the region and other settings as required.

  4. Develop File Transfer Logic:

    • Write the code to handle the file transfer from SAP ECC to the S3 bucket.
    • This could be a custom ABAP program, a Java application, or a script that uses the AWS SDK to upload files.

  5. Testing:

    • Test the file transfer in a non-production environment to ensure that the setup works as expected.
    • Verify that the files are being uploaded to the S3 bucket and that error handling is in place.

  6. Monitoring and Logging:

    • Implement logging and monitoring to track the file transfer process.
    • You can use AWS CloudWatch to monitor the S3 bucket and set up alerts for any issues.

  7. Security:

    • Ensure that the data is encrypted in transit using HTTPS.
    • Consider encrypting the data at rest in the S3 bucket.

  8. Scheduling:

    • If the file transfer needs to happen on a schedule, set up a job in SAP ECC to trigger the transfer process.

  9. Error Handling:

    • Implement robust error handling and retry mechanisms in your file transfer logic.

  10. Documentation:

    • Document the setup and the process for future reference and maintenance.

Please note that the specifics of the setup can vary based on the version of SAP ECC, the programming language used, and the AWS SDK version. Always refer to the latest documentation for the AWS SDK and SAP ECC for detailed instructions.

entry.title: Re: Regarding HR module
entry.content: Hello Torben, Thank you, for the information. With the ad on pack can we manage Master data, ESS, MSS, Time Management and Payroll.

entry.title: Re: table relation between lqua and prps (urgent)
entry.content:
Hi, 

For this conversion you can use these functions:

L_SONUM_CONV_INT_EXT  - Conversion of the LVS special inventory number from internal to L_SONUM_CONV_EXT_INT - Conversion of the LVS special inventory number from external to internal

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Error: 500 (); GET /nsDemoSampleApp/com.sap.edm.sampleservice.v2/ Internal Server Error
entry.content:
Hi BIll,
I have been also facing the same issue. I am not using any periods in my name.

"Failed to initialize application data service - Error: 500 (); GET /nsDemoSampleApp/SampleService4/ Internal Server Error
Customer"
Can u please suggest where should i look into? I works fine on mobile.

Thanks
Abhishek Chauhan

entry.title: Re: Error: 500 (); GET /nsDemoSampleApp/com.sap.edm.sampleservice.v2/ Internal Server Error
entry.content: Hi Bill,

entry.title: Re: How to maintain the Single Project definition ID for...
entry.content:
Hi Niyaz,

We are unclear with your requirement. from the query title it's understood that yo are looking out for a intercompany scenario. 

We have an intercompany scenario (4AU) delivered as part best practices in S/4HANA Cloud,Public Edition and it is as shown below. check the scenarios and decide on if it fits your requirements.

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2402/SolP/4AU?region=DE

Regards,

Sai

entry.title: Re: Best approach to compare HANA Tables
entry.content:
Thanks @FrankRiesner for the response. Do you mean the graphical CV with Join condition on 60 dimensions b/w t1,t2 ( lets say out of 100 columns 60 are Dims , 40 are measures ) would be much faster than the comparison using MINUS approach ? As I wanted to do the data comparison for each column including DIMs as well along with measures .

Thanks

Kiran

entry.title: Re: How can i adjust the HU_LABEL in the public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

if you want to change the print layout, then you could use Adobe LiveCycle Designer.
You could check the scope item 1LQ for Output Management.

Best regards,
Barış

entry.title: Re: Calculated dimensions based on several objects
entry.content: Really?! Is this simple logical dilemma not possible in webi?

entry.title: Re: Regarding HR module
entry.content:
Hi Rini,

you will be able to manage all employee data and cover ESS / MSS use cases. The exact functionalities covered can be found in the feature scope description of SuccessFactors Employee Central: https://help.sap.com/doc/7dace3fd20d948999f116d8b94a175bb/2405/en-US/SAP%20SuccessFactors%20Employee%20Central%201H%202024.pdf

entry.title: Re: How to create an import connection to SAP BW using SNC in SAC
entry.content:
Hello  @ivanyin 

Can we use SNC to secure a data import connection from SAC to on-premise SAP S/4HANA? If not, what are other ways to secure this connection?

Many Thanks!

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Regarding HR module
entry.content: You will however need an additional license to license your Payroll. This would be "SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll", the feature scope description can be found here: https://help.sap.com/doc/a017ff2a018e4cd893fddbb5c52a4530/2405/en-US/SAP%20SuccessFactors%20Employee%20Central%20Payroll%201H%202024.pdf

entry.title: Re: Business Integration Builder (BIB) Knowledge
entry.content:
Dear All,

 

I am new to BIB and learning now.  Is there any one help on end to end steps including middle ware (CPI) settings.   Pleae do needful.

 

Regards,Jay

entry.title: 手形と前受/前払金の特殊仕訳取引が定義されていませんMsg 番号 FP030
Calculated member in cross tab report
entry.content:
Hello,

I need help with performing calculations using crosstabs in Crystal Reports.

 
This Gross Margin at the bottom  is the calculated member
this is the formula that is used to  budget
GridValueAt(GetIndexOf("Command.U_Sub","Sales"), CurrentColumnIndex, CurrentSummaryIndex)-
GridValueAt(GetIndexOf("Command.U_Sub","Cost of Raw Materials"), CurrentColumnIndex, CurrentSummaryIndex)
this is the formula that is used to calculate total Actual 
GridValueAt(GetIndexOf("Command.U_Sub","Sales"), CurrentColumnIndex, CurrentSummaryIndex)-
GridValueAt(GetIndexOf("Command.U_Sub","Cost of Raw Materials"), CurrentColumnIndex, CurrentSummaryIndex)
this is the formula that is used to calculate total Variance
GridValueAt(GetIndexOf("Command.U_Sub","Sales"), CurrentColumnIndex, CurrentSummaryIndex)-
GridValueAt(GetIndexOf("Command.U_Sub","Cost of Raw Materials"), CurrentColumnIndex, CurrentSummaryIndex)
 
Now I want to calculate the total variance% by dividing the total variance by the total budget value.
(259,160.47)/455,926.85=(0.57).How can I do this?

entry.title: Re: Abap program to end specific User's Program, by sesion/tcode or GUIID
entry.content: ohh apologies, my reading comprehension is worse than I thought.

entry.title: SAP Fiori App F1311A My Leave Requests: missing team calender
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are missing the team calender in the app mentions above. It was available in the WebDynpro Apps. In our opinion it is important to check with the absences of the colleagues. They are neither on the start screen nor on the create screen available. 

Does anyone know, why it is not available or if it's possible to make it available? 

Thanks in advance!
Best regards, Chris
